title: Libpod Networking Support
body: Current state of networking support. Containers can request a network namespace be created for them. Firewalls are not handled right now, and it's not well tested. Also needs work so system restarts will be handled properly.

comments.body: Going to rebase on top of #94 - need the restart handling code
Rebased on top of #94 and added some commits to wire networking into kpod run and handle system restart
Tests are failing because CNI plugins & configs are not installed
Tests are green, but Papr still isn't installing the CNI plugins so i strongly suspect network-related tests will fail there.

I managed to break the tests again, but code-wise this should be enough to get the backend working. Still needs work to wire it into kpod run, but I'll probably pass it off to @baude for that. Removing WIP label.
Papr failures are a result of not installing the net plugins there. Travis seems to be an issue with the test network.

so this doesn't fix the `--network=default` flag for `kpod run`, am i right? it only allows interoperability with CNI-made config files? are there plans to add that commandline flag soon?

is there a tracking issue for `--network`? #129 maybe?
@anarcat This PR is for enabling network when --net != host ... I'll be working on the host part here soon.
@anarcat and then port binding will be next I think.
that makes sense. i guess it makes sense to followup on #129 then.. thanks!
